title: The SQuAD Benchmark
concept: The Stanford Question Answering Dataset (SQuAD) is the standard benchmark for training and evaluating QA systems, measuring Exact Match (EM) and F1 scores.

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym SQuAD stand for?
exercises.explain: SQuAD stands for the Stanford Question Answering Dataset, developed by Stanford University to evaluate NLP models.

cards.title: Testing AI Comprehension
cards.body: How do we know if an AI actually understands what it reads, or if it is just guessing words? We put it through SQuAD—the ultimate reading comprehension test for artificial intelligence!

cards.title: What is SQuAD?
cards.body: SQuAD (Stanford Question Answering Dataset) is a widely used benchmark collection of questions and answers based on Wikipedia articles. AI models are evaluated on how accurately they can extract the correct answer directly from the text.

cards.title: The Reading Comprehension Test
cards.body: Think of SQuAD like a standardized reading comprehension exam for humans. Instead of grading how creative a student is, the teacher checks if they found the exact right sentence in the passage to answer the question, grading them on precision and thoroughness.

cards.title: SQuAD in Action
cards.body: If the text says, 'The Super Bowl was played in Santa Clara, California,' and the question is 'Where was the Super Bowl played?', the model must extract 'Santa Clara, California'. If it outputs exactly that, it gets a perfect Exact Match (EM) score of 100%!

cards.title: Not a Creative Writing Test
cards.body: Many believe SQuAD tests an AI's ability to write long, conversational explanations. In reality, it mostly tests extractive reading—finding the precise start and end points of the answer within a provided text block.

cards.title: EM vs. F1 Score
cards.body: SQuAD grades models using two different ways of looking at correctness.

left.text: The AI's answer must match the human key exactly, letter for letter.

right.text: Measures the shared words between the AI's answer and the correct answer.

exercises.prompt: Which two primary metrics are used to grade AI performance on the SQuAD benchmark?
exercises.explain: SQuAD uses two main metrics: Exact Match (EM), which requires a perfect character-for-character match, and F1 Score, which measures the overlap of words between the predicted and correct answers.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Some versions of the SQuAD dataset include questions that have no answer in the provided text.
exercises.explain: SQuAD 2.0 introduced questions that cannot be answered based on the text, forcing models to recognize when the information is missing.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The SQuAD metric that requires the AI's answer to perfectly match the correct answer word-for-word is called _______.
exercises.sentence: The metric that demands a perfect character-for-character match is called Exact Match.
exercises.explain: Exact Match (EM) requires the AI's answer to be character-for-character identical to the human-annotated ground truth.

exercises.prompt: Given the text 'Super Bowl 50 was played in Santa Clara, California,' which system successfully extracted the SQuAD-style answer to 'Where was Super Bowl 50 played?'

options.text: System A: 'It was played in Los Angeles.'
System C: 'I think it took place in California near San Jose.'

exercises.explain: System B got the exact text span 'Santa Clara, California' which matches the text, whereas System A failed by guessing an incorrect city, and System C generated a conversational response rather than extracting the exact span.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of the SQuAD benchmark in AI evaluation?

options.text: To test reading comprehension by having models extract the exact text span that answers a question.
To evaluate how fast a model can generate creative fictional stories from scratch.
To measure how well an AI can translate text between ten different languages.
To test if an AI can write clean Python code using algorithms.

exercises.explain: SQuAD tests an AI's ability to read a passage and extract the exact span of text that answers a question. It is evaluated using Exact Match (EM) and F1 scores.
